task.objective: Enhance the `compare_executions` MCP tool to include performance regression/improvement categorization for each test. While duration delta is already calculated, the enhancement adds semantic labels to help AI assistants quickly identify performance issues.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - MCP Server Tools
context.background:
The `compare_executions` tool compares two test runs and returns test-level differences. It currently returns status comparison (new_failure, fixed, etc.) and raw duration deltas: - durationDeltaMs: Absolute difference in milliseconds - durationDeltaPercent: Percentage change (can be positive or negative)
However, it lacks semantic categorization for performance changes. Without this: - AI assistants must interpret raw numbers to understand significance - No threshold-based classification (what constitutes a "regression"?) - Harder to filter or prioritize performance issues
The optimization analysis suggests adding: - durationCategory: "regression" | "improvement" | "stable" - Based on configurable thresholds (e.g., >20% slower = regression)
context.audience: - AI assistants comparing build performance - Developers investigating test slowdowns - CI/CD pipelines detecting performance regressions
context.impact: MEDIUM - Provides actionable performance insights without additional processing. Especially useful for detecting tests that got significantly slower after code changes.

existing_code.file:
{ name: "compare_executions", description: `Compare two test executions to identify regressions, improvements, and changes. Side-by-side analysis of test results.
Use baseline_id/current_id for specific executions, or baseline_branch/current_branch for branch comparison (uses latest execution from each).
Returns: - baseline: Execution metadata (id, branch, commit, status, test counts) - current: Execution metadata - summary: Pass rate delta, duration delta, test count delta, status change counts - tests: Array of test comparisons with diff categories
Diff Categories: - new_failure: Was passing in baseline, now failing - fixed: Was failing in baseline, now passing - new_test: Only exists in current execution - removed_test: Only exists in baseline execution - unchanged: Same status in both
Use filter param to focus on specific categories (e.g., filter="new_failure" to see only regressions).`, inputSchema: { type: "object" as const, properties: { baseline_id: { ... }, current_id: { ... }, ... }, }, }

// Already calculates duration data: SELECT ... b.duration_ms as "baselineDurationMs", c.duration_ms as "currentDurationMs", (c.duration_ms - b.duration_ms) as "durationDeltaMs", CASE WHEN b.duration_ms > 0 THEN ROUND(((c.duration_ms - b.duration_ms)::float / b.duration_ms * 100))::integer ELSE NULL END as "durationDeltaPercent", CASE WHEN b.test_signature IS NULL THEN 'new_test' WHEN c.test_signature IS NULL THEN 'removed_test' WHEN b.status = 'passed' AND c.status = 'failed' THEN 'new_failure' WHEN b.status = 'failed' AND c.status = 'passed' THEN 'fixed' ELSE 'unchanged' END as "diffCategory" FROM baseline_tests b FULL OUTER JOIN current_tests c ON b.test_signature = c.test_signature

issue.improvement: Add durationCategory field with human-readable labels

issue.actual: Only test-level data; no aggregate performance stats
issue.improvement: Add performanceSummary with count of regressions/improvements

functional.requirement: Use sensible defaults: >20% slower = regression, <-20% faster = improvement
Add performance_threshold parameter to customize thresholds
Add performanceSummary to response with regression/improvement counts

non_functional.requirement: No additional database queries (compute in SQL or post-process)
Maintain backwards compatibility with existing fields

solution.approach: 1. Add durationCategory to SQL CASE statement in compareExecutions 2. Add performance_threshold parameter to tool schema 3. Add performanceSummary to response 4. Optionally add performance_regressions filter value
solution.updated_sql: -- Add new CASE for duration category CASE WHEN b.test_signature IS NULL THEN NULL -- New test, no comparison WHEN c.test_signature IS NULL THEN NULL -- Removed test, no comparison WHEN b.duration_ms IS NULL OR b.duration_ms = 0 THEN NULL -- No baseline duration WHEN ((c.duration_ms - b.duration_ms)::float / b.duration_ms * 100) > 20 THEN 'regression' WHEN ((c.duration_ms - b.duration_ms)::float / b.duration_ms * 100) < -20 THEN 'improvement' ELSE 'stable' END as "durationCategory"

steps.step: 1. Start dev server: npm run dev
2. Compare two executions with known duration differences
3. Verify durationCategory is present for each test
4. Verify performanceSummary counts match manual calculation
5. Test filter="performance_regression" to see only slow tests
6. Test with performance_threshold=10 for stricter classification

test.script:
# Test 1: Default comparison curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "compare_executions", "args": {"baseline_id": 1, "current_id": 2}}'
# Verify response includes durationCategory and performanceSummary
# Test 2: Filter performance regressions curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "compare_executions", "args": {"baseline_id": 1, "current_id": 2, "filter": "performance_regression"}}'
# Test 3: Custom threshold curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "compare_executions", "args": {"baseline_id": 1, "current_id": 2, "performance_threshold": 10}}'

implementation_order.step: Update TestComparisonItem interface to include durationCategory
Add durationCategory CASE to compareExecutions SQL query
Add performanceThreshold parameter to compareExecutions function
Update handler with performanceSummary and new filter

always.rule: Return null for durationCategory when baseline has no duration data
Apply symmetric thresholds (20% slower = regression, 20% faster = improvement)

never.rule: Never classify new/removed tests as having performance changes

documentation_updates.description: The MCP documentation page must be updated to reflect the new performance-related response fields (durationCategory, performanceSummary) and the new filter option.

update.current_code: { name: "compare_executions", description: "Compare two test executions side-by-side to identify regressions, improvements, and changes.", category: "performance" as const, parameters: [ { name: "baseline_id", type: "number", description: "Baseline execution ID" }, { name: "current_id", type: "number", description: "Current execution ID" }, { name: "baseline_branch", type: "string", description: "Use latest execution from this branch as baseline" }, { name: "current_branch", type: "string", description: "Use latest execution from this branch as current" }, { name: "suite", type: "string", description: "Filter to specific suite (for branch lookups)" }, { name: "filter", type: "string", description: "Filter: new_failure | fixed | new_test | removed_test | all" }, ], responseFields: [ "baseline, current: Execution metadata (id, branch, commit, status)", "summary: Pass rate delta, duration delta, test count changes", "tests: Array of test comparisons with diff categories", "Diff categories: new_failure, fixed, new_test, removed_test, unchanged", ], example: `"Compare the last two runs on main branch"`, },
update.updated_code: { name: "compare_executions", description: "Compare two test executions side-by-side to identify regressions, improvements, and performance changes.", category: "performance" as const, parameters: [ { name: "baseline_id", type: "number", description: "Baseline execution ID" }, { name: "current_id", type: "number", description: "Current execution ID" }, { name: "baseline_branch", type: "string", description: "Use latest execution from this branch as baseline" }, { name: "current_branch", type: "string", description: "Use latest execution from this branch as current" }, { name: "suite", type: "string", description: "Filter to specific suite (for branch lookups)" }, { name: "filter", type: "string", description: "Filter: new_failure | fixed | new_test | removed_test | performance_regression | all" }, { name: "performance_threshold", type: "number", default: "20", description: "% change threshold for regression/improvement" }, ], responseFields: [ "baseline, current: Execution metadata (id, branch, commit, status)", "summary: Pass rate delta, duration delta, test count changes", "performanceSummary: Count of regressions, improvements, stable tests", "tests: Array with diff categories and duration categories", "Diff categories: new_failure, fixed, new_test, removed_test, unchanged", "Duration categories: regression, improvement, stable", ], example: `"Compare the last two runs and show performance regressions"`, },

update.note: Update the /api/compare endpoint documentation to include performanceSummary in the response example and document the new performance_threshold parameter.

files_to_modify.file: lib/mcp/tools.ts - Update tool definition and handler

output_format.complexity: Low-Medium - Mostly adding a SQL CASE and response processing
